data.title: Studio desk archive naming convention and filtering system

data.body: Convention: <date>.<type>.<tag>.<project?>.<size>.ext where date=MM.DD.YY, type=moodboard|prototype|demo|sketch|screenshot|visual|texture|recording, tag=research|code|ai|design|brand|architecture|writing, project=optional kebab-case, size=xs|sm|md|lg|xl. Dots are parseable for future filter component. Rename existing studio-desk-archive files, update all MDX frontmatter refs, update /update-site skill to enforce convention on new entries.

Additional convention for ~/Documents/Artifacts/raw/<project>/: files should be named <stage>.<n>.ext where stage=reference|development|final. Reference = inspiration/things not made by Julianna (use sparingly on site, always attributed). Development = process shots, prototypes, testing, iteration. Final = finished product, hero-worthy. This stage label informs what gets pulled into production and how it's used.
